message.content:
你是调研 finder。任务背景:MAformac 项目(纯端侧 macOS SwiftUI 演示助手,主演示面已定为 macOS,后续要做 UIUE 前置化)。
要求:
- 先用 ToolSearch 加载 WebSearch 和 WebFetch,然后联网搜索。
- 每条 findings 的 claim 必须来自你真实打开(WebFetch)或搜索摘要确认过的来源;禁止凭记忆编造 URL、数字、版本号。能 WebFetch 的关键来源尽量实访,并把 fetched 标 true。
- 优先 2024-2026 的新资料(Liquid Glass / macOS 26 时代),老经典(HIG 长期原则、Nielsen 启发式)也可收但注明。
- 输出全部用中文(URL/API 名/专有名词保留英文)。
- 8-15 条高质量 findings,宁缺毋滥。
调研角度:设计流程方法论——design system 从零构建流程、Figma(或其他工具)→ SwiftUI 的 handoff 工作流、低保真→高保真原型迭代方法、design sprint/双钻模型等通用方法论在桌面端 App 的裁剪应用、solo 开发者/小团队的轻量设计流程。

attachment.content:
- archive-research-pack: Use when 一次多路 workflow 调研 / deep-research / pre-mortem oracle 搜证收口,需要把成果落盘防失忆。症状:调研完只想存一个总报告、transcript 在 /tmp 待清会丢、finder 引的 source URL 事后无法复核、各路一手档与综合报告混在一起。
- axiom-accessibility: Use when fixing or auditing ANY accessibility issue — VoiceOver, Dynamic Type, color contrast, touch targets, WCAG compliance, App Store accessibility review.
- axiom-ai: Use when implementing ANY Apple Intelligence or on-device AI feature. Covers Foundation Models, @Generable, LanguageModelSession, structured output, Tool protocol, iOS 26 AI integration.
- axiom-apple-docs: Use when ANY question involves Apple framework APIs, Swift compiler errors, or Xcode-bundled documentation. Covers Liquid Glass, Swift 6.2 concurrency, Foundation Models, SwiftData, StoreKit, 32 Swift compiler diagnostics.
- axiom-build: Use when ANY iOS build fails, test crashes, Xcode misbehaves, or environment issue occurs before debugging code. Covers build failures, compilation errors, dependency conflicts, simulator problems, environment-first diagnostics.
- axiom-concurrency: Use when writing ANY async code, actors, threads, or seeing ANY concurrency error. Covers Swift 6 concurrency, @MainActor, Sendable, data races, async/await patterns.
- axiom-data: Use when working with ANY data persistence, database, storage, CloudKit, migration, or serialization. Covers SwiftData, Core Data, GRDB, SQLite, CloudKit sync, file storage, Codable, migrations.
- axiom-design: Use when making design decisions, implementing HIG patterns, Liquid Glass, SF Symbols, typography, or structuring app entry points and authentication flows.
- axiom-games: Use when building ANY 2D or 3D game with SpriteKit, SceneKit, or RealityKit, or adding touch controls or game controller support. Covers scene graphs, ECS, physics, actions, game loops, rendering, TouchController, GCController.
- axiom-graphics: Use when working with ANY GPU rendering, Metal, OpenGL migration, shaders, 3D content, RealityKit, AR, USD/USDZ files, or display performance. Covers Metal migration, shader conversion, RealityKit ECS, RealityView, USDKit, ProMotion.
- axiom-health: Use when working with HealthKit, WorkoutKit, health data, workouts, or fitness features on iOS or watchOS. Covers permissions, queries, background delivery, custom workouts, multidevice coordination.
- axiom-integration: Use when integrating ANY iOS system feature - Siri, Shortcuts, widgets, IAP, localization, privacy, alarms, calendar, reminders, contacts, background tasks, push notifications, timers. Covers App Intents, WidgetKit, StoreKit, EventKit, Contacts.
- axiom-location: Use when implementing location services, maps, geofencing, or debugging location/MapKit issues. Covers Core Location, CLMonitor, MapKit, annotations, directions.
- axiom-macos: Use when building ANY macOS app — windows, menus, sandboxing, distribution, AppKit bridging or modernization (control events, state restoration, concentric corners), or macOS-specific SwiftUI patterns.
- axiom-media: Use when working with camera, photos, audio, haptics, ShazamKit, or Now Playing. Covers AVCaptureSession, PHPicker, PhotosPicker, AVFoundation, Core Haptics, audio recognition, MediaPlayer, CarPlay, MusicKit.
- axiom-networking: Use when implementing or debugging ANY network connection, API call, or socket. Covers URLSession, Network.framework, NetworkConnection, connection diagnostics.
- axiom-payments: Use when accepting ANY real-world payment — Apple Pay, Wallet passes, Tap to Pay, Orders in Wallet. NOT in-app purchase / digital content (use axiom-integration). Covers entitlements, certs, signing, App Review payment rules.
- axiom-performance: Use when app feels slow, memory grows, battery drains, or diagnosing ANY performance issue. Covers memory leaks, profiling, Instruments workflows, retain cycles, performance optimization.
- axiom-security: Use when storing credentials securely, encrypting data, implementing passkeys, securing AI/agentic features against prompt injection, code signing, or managing certificates and provisioning profiles.
- axiom-shipping: Use when preparing ANY app for submission, handling App Store rejections, writing appeals, or managing App Store Connect. Covers submission checklists, rejection troubleshooting, metadata requirements, privacy manifests, age ratings, export compliance.
- axiom-swift: Use when reviewing Swift code for modern idioms, working with noncopyable types, implementing drag and drop, adding debug deep links, or building for tvOS.
- axiom-swiftui: Use when building, fixing, or improving ANY SwiftUI UI — views, navigation, layout, animations, performance, architecture, gestures, debugging, iOS 26 features.
- axiom-testing: Use when writing ANY test, debugging flaky tests, making tests faster, or choosing Swift Testing vs XCTest. Covers unit tests, UI tests, async testing, test architecture.
- axiom-tools: Use when asking how to use Axiom or what skills exist, capturing console with xclog, symbolicating .ips/MetricKit/.crash crashes with xcsym, driving/validating simulator UI & accessibility with xcui, or analyzing xctrace/CPU profiles with xcprof.
- axiom-uikit: Use when bridging UIKit and SwiftUI, modernizing UIKit apps (scene lifecycle, resizability), debugging Auto Layout, Combine, TextKit, PencilKit, or UIKit animations.
- axiom-vision: Use when implementing ANY computer vision feature — image analysis, pose detection, person segmentation, subject lifting, text recognition, barcode scanning.
- axiom-watchos: Use when building ANY watchOS app — app structure, independent apps, Watch Connectivity, Smart Stack widgets, complications, controls, RelevanceKit, background tasks, ClockKit migration.
- axiom-xcode-mcp: Use when connecting to Xcode via MCP, using xcrun mcpbridge, or working with ANY Xcode MCP tool (XcodeRead, BuildProject, RunTests, RenderPreview). Covers setup, tool reference, workflow patterns, troubleshooting.
- closeout-receipt-writer: Use when 一个 apply/重型任务/审计 wave 收口需要写 receipt 证据。症状:想用 hardcoded pass、读 metadata flag 自证 verdict、凭印象写状态、或 receipt 与实跑 log 脱节;0-34 灾难根因正是 receipt 读自己的 metadata flag 翻 pass、零 token 证据。
- doc-cascade-sweep: Use when 范式翻案/架构大改/口径回写后,grep 全仓命中几十个文件含过期锚点或旧数字,需要全仓级联更新。症状:看到 grep 命中 60+ 文件就想 sed 全量替换、或一个决策只 append 进一个文件没回写文档组、或改了带日期的历史快照正文破坏一手溯源。
- ios-debugger-agent: Build, run, and debug iOS apps on Simulator with XcodeBuildMCP. Use when launching an app, inspecting simulator UI or logs, or diagnosing runtime behavior.
- ios-ettrace-performance: Capture and interpret iOS Simulator ETTrace profiles. Use when profiling launch or runtime latency, comparing traces, or finding CPU-heavy stacks.
- ios-simulator-skill: 29 production-ready scripts for iOS app testing, building, and automation. Provides semantic UI navigation, build automation, accessibility testing, and simulator lifecycle management. Optimized for AI agents with minimal token output.
- openspec-apply-change: Implement tasks from an OpenSpec change. Use when the user wants to start implementing, continue implementation, or work through tasks.
- openspec-archive-change: Archive a completed change in the experimental workflow. Use when the user wants to finalize and archive a change after implementation is complete.
- openspec-explore: Enter explore mode - a thinking partner for exploring ideas, investigating problems, and clarifying requirements. Use when the user wants to think through something before or during a change.
- openspec-propose: Propose a new change with all artifacts generated in one step. Use when the user wants to quickly describe what they want to build and get a complete proposal with design, specs, and tasks ready for implementation.
- openspec-sync-specs: Sync delta specs from a change to main specs. Use when the user wants to update main specs with changes from a delta spec, without archiving the change.
- verify-external-claims: Use when 一个多路 workflow/finder 调研、deep-research、或综合官报告引用了 arxiv ID、精确数字（调用数/百分比/star/benchmark）、或 repo 活跃度断言,且这些要驱动决策。症状:finder 在搜证压力下高发编造精确数字 + 引用 ID,且常安到真实存在的 source 上(issue/论文真、数字假),综合官即使做了本机 grep 也漏外部声称。
- opsx:apply: Implement tasks from an OpenSpec change (Experimental)
- opsx:archive: Archive a completed change in the experimental workflow
- opsx:explore: Enter explore mode - think through ideas, investigate problems, clarify requirements
- opsx:propose: Propose a new change - create it and generate all artifacts in one step
- opsx:sync: Sync delta specs from a change to main specs
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Links: [{"title":"Figma to SwiftUI","url":"https://www.builder.io/blog/figma-to-swiftui"},{"title":"Figma to iOS: Convert designs to mobile apps in seconds","url":"https://www.builder.io/blog/convert-figma-to-ios"},{"title":"Trace: Convert Figma Designs to SwiftUI & Preview live apps | Figma","url":"https://www.figma.com/community/plugin/1339121057234376529/trace-convert-figma-designs-to-swiftui-preview-live-apps"},{"title":"Figma to SwiftUI - DEV Community","url":"https://dev.to/builderio/figma-to-swiftui-10mb"},{"title":"Figma Designs to Swift Code: A Streamlined Workflow - DEV Community","url":"https://dev.to/atforeveryoung/figma-designs-to-swift-code-a-streamlined-workflow-27f0"},{"title":"Figma to SwiftUI: Design to Native iOS Code","url":"https://codia.ai/docs/getting-started/figma-to-swiftui.html"},{"title":"Connecting SwiftUI components | Developer Docs","url":"https://developers.figma.com/docs/code-connect/swiftui/"},{"title":"Figma to Code: Flutter, React, Next.js, HTML, SwiftUI and ...","url":"https://www.dhiwise.com/post/figma-to-code-with-dhiwise"},{"title":"figma · GitHub Topics · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/topics/figma?l=swift&o=desc&s=updated"}]

Based on the search results, here's what's currently available for Figma to SwiftUI handoff workflows in 2025:

## AI-Powered Code Generation Tools

The Visual Copilot plugin now features a powerful CLI workflow perfect for iOS developers, allowing you to export Figma designs directly into your project whether using SwiftUI, React Native, or Flutter, with a single command handling downloading your design, translating it into the right framework's code, and inserting it into your codebase automatically.

For developers using AI-assisted editors like Cursor or Windsurf, this workflow makes design handoffs nearly invisible, integrating Figma designs into your iOS project as a seamless, automated part of your development routine, letting you stay focused on building great app features while the CLI handles initial UI scaffolding and significantly speeding up the iteration cycle from design to functional iOS UI.

Other tools like Trace help you convert Figma designs to live SwiftUI prototypes within minutes, using AI to write SwiftUI code and compile the entire project within minutes. Additionally, Codia AI's Figma plugin converts designs to SwiftUI code, enhancing workflow efficiency and supporting full-stack mobile app development.

## Figma Code Connect for SwiftUI

Code Connect helps you connect your SwiftUI components with Figma components, covering basic setup to get your first code snippets in Dev Mode, followed by prop mapping and variant mapping.

## Best Practices for Handoff

Using Figma's auto layout feature to organize elements into responsive hierarchies is the single most impactful action for ensuring a smooth import.

Links: [{"title":"Build A Design System from Scratch in 7 Steps | UXPin","url":"https://www.uxpin.com/studio/blog/build-a-design-system-from-scratch-in-7-steps/"},{"title":"How to Build a Design System from Scratch - A Comprehensive Step-by-Step Guide","url":"https://moldstud.com/articles/p-how-to-build-a-design-system-from-scratch-a-comprehensive-step-by-step-guide"},{"title":"How to Build Design Systems From Scratch?","url":"https://www.netguru.com/blog/how-to-build-design-systems-from-scratch"},{"title":"Design Systems: Step-by-Step Guide to Creating Your Own","url":"https://www.uxpin.com/create-design-system-guide/"},{"title":"How to Build Your Startup’s First Design System | by Maya Brennan | Bootcamp | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/design-bootcamp/how-to-build-your-startups-first-design-system-4d8724fbb802"},{"title":"Building a Design System from Scratch: A Practical Guide","url":"https://designkit.tech/blog/building-design-system-from-scratch"},{"title":"Building Design Systems: Complete 2026 Guide","url":"https://inhaq.com/blog/building-design-systems-from-scratch-a-design-engineers-guide"},{"title":"How to Create Your First Design System in 5 Steps","url":"https://designsystems.surf/articles/5-steps-to-creating-a-design-system-from-scratch"},{"title":"Building a design system from scratch","url":"https://www.nagarro.com/en/blog/building-a-design-system-from-scratch"},{"title":"How to Set Up a Design System in 2025 ✨ | by Pierluigi Giglio | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@pierluigi.giglio.01_23202/how-to-set-up-a-design-system-in-2025-4fd9cc165d99"}]

I've found comprehensive search results about building design systems from scratch. Here are the key findings:

## Key Steps for Building a Design System from Scratch

The best place to start is evaluating what you already have - which elements do you like, which do you want to replace, and where are there inconsistencies between products or pages.

The process includes creating a UI inventory, getting organizational buy-in, building a multidisciplinary team, establishing rules and principles, building color palettes, typographic scales, implementing icon libraries, standardizing style properties, and building the first design system pattern.

## Foundation Elements

Start with the basics: colors, typography, and measurements, which shape tokens like spacing, radii, and text hierarchy. Create a master list of all your design choices, whether choosing among existing elements or creating them from scratch, and rectify inconsistencies before adding them to the master.

## Timeline and Approach

For one specific project, it took around three and a half months to deliver design system documentation, with approximately one and a half months on a full-time basis and two months at 50% workload. Building design system patterns is an iterative process - do not focus on building all patterns at once, instead start by identifying the best architecture and build it one by one.

## Business Benefits

Teams using design systems ship 34% faster and reduce design-related bugs by 47%.

## Important Consideration

Building a design system from scratch is one thing, maintaining and improving it is another - there's no such thing as a finished design system, only iterations, as products evolve.

I've completed the web search for your query about the double diamond design process, lightweight approaches, and small teams in 2024. Here are the key findings:

## Overview of the Double Diamond for Small Teams

The Double Diamond framework has been successfully used for small solo projects lasting just a few days to small team projects spanning 3–6 months. The approach should not be prescriptive or followed to the letter — it is a guide or reference.

## Lightweight Approaches for Resource-Constrained Teams

For teams with limited resources, the framework can be streamlined:

- A lightweight Discover/Define phase can be committed to with a firm decision date and clear metrics to test
- Discovery should be limited rather than skipped entirely, since a few days of targeted evidence can prevent months of building the wrong thing
- Even small teams can benefit from the Double Diamond design process principles by prioritizing key phases and leveraging lightweight tools like rapid prototyping

## Key Adaptations for 2024

The available tools in 2024 are quite different from what designers had in 2005, with artificial intelligence having grown by leaps and bounds. In 2024, collaboration is key, with advanced collaboration tools, augmented reality interfaces, and virtual reality environments enhancing the creative process.

The search results include practical guidance on how to adapt the Double Diamond framework for different team sizes and project timelines.

I've completed the web search on low fidelity to high fidelity prototyping iteration workflow best practices for 2025. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Key Best Practices for Low to High Fidelity Prototyping Workflows

**Strategic Progression:**
Start every project with low-fidelity prototype work to validate fundamental concepts quickly and inexpensively. Use medium-fidelity prototype development to resolve structural issues before investing in detailed design. Reserve high-fidelity prototype creation for finalizing designs and preparing for development handoff.

**When to Transition Between Fidelity Levels:**
When you can clearly articulate what specific question you're testing, and that question requires fidelity to answer, it's time to move up. Until then, stay lo-fi and keep iterating fast.

**Core Workflow Principles:**
Define clear objectives: Before you start building, know exactly what you want to learn from your prototype. Are you testing a user flow, a specific feature, or the overall concept? This clarity prevents "prototype creep" and wasted effort.

**Efficient Iteration:**
You can edit artifacts in the middle of a flow and rerun only the downstream steps, not the whole thing. The result reflects the new input without starting over. This is how you iterate fast without losing context.

**Speed Advantage:**
Teams can sketch multiple approaches to a checkout flow in the same time it takes to build a single high-fidelity version.

**Testing & Collaboration:**
Test with real users: The most valuable feedback comes from your target audience. Even a few users can provide significant insights that guide your next iteration.

A Complete Guide (2026)","url":"https://www.uxpin.com/studio/blog/what-are-design-tokens/"},{"title":"Design Systems & Design Tokens Explained — design.dev","url":"https://design.dev/guides/design-systems/"},{"title":"Atomic Design with SwiftUI: Building a Themed System | by isa nur fajar | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@ishafajar11/atomic-design-with-swiftui-building-a-themed-system-72f5721c25bc"}]

I've found comprehensive information about design tokens in SwiftUI. Here's what the search results reveal:

## What Are Design Tokens?

Design tokens are platform-agnostic variables that store design-related values such as colors, typography, spacing, and component styles. More specifically, design tokens are named, semantic values, not raw numbers, and they express intent, not implementation.

## Single Source of Truth

Design tokens serve as a single source of truth for all visual decisions. Design tokens create a single source of truth for design systems, enhance collaboration and ensure seamless execution from design to development. This is particularly important because every color comes from the design system, nowhere else.

## Types of Design Tokens

The three levels are: (1) Global tokens — raw values like color hex codes and pixel sizes, (2) Alias tokens — semantic references like color-primary that point to global tokens, and (3) Component tokens — scoped values like button-background-color that point to alias tokens.

## Implementation in SwiftUI

In SwiftUI, design tokens allow designers and developers to define and manage values in a centralized and systematic way, instead of hardcoding values directly into XML, SwiftUI, or design files.

Designers can standardize UI elements by defining a clear set of colors, typography, and spacing values that remain consistent across all screens and components. Typography Tokens create heading, subheading, and body text styles using predefined font sizes and weights, while Spacing Tokens set standard padding, margins, and grid spacing values.

## Workflow Benefits

CI/CD pipelines can push updated tokens into codebases automatically, reducing manual effort and human error. When design values are updated, a single token update reflects across hundreds of UI components globally, saving time, reducing errors, and ensuring cross-platform consistency.

I've successfully retrieved search results for your query about Apple Liquid Glass design, macOS 26, and app design guidelines. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Overview

Liquid Glass is a design language developed by Apple as a unified visual theme for the graphical user interfaces for its suite of operating systems, announced on June 9, 2025, at the Worldwide Developers Conference (WWDC).

## Design Features

Liquid Glass is translucent and behaves like glass in the real world, with color informed by surrounding content and intelligently adapts between light and dark environments. Born out of a close collaboration between the design and engineering teams, Liquid Glass uses real-time rendering and dynamically reacts to movement with specular highlights.

## Platform Implementation

The new design extends across iOS 26, iPadOS 26, macOS Tahoe 26, watchOS 26, and tvOS 26 to establish even more harmony while maintaining the distinct qualities that make each platform unique.

## For macOS Specifically

In macOS Tahoe 26, users can customize the desktop and Dock with widgets and app icons that come to life in light or dark appearances, colorful new light and dark tints, as well as an elegant new clear look. And it's made even better with a completely transparent menu bar that makes the Mac display feel even larger.

## Developer Resources

For developers using SwiftUI, UIKit, and AppKit, an updated set of APIs make it easy to adopt the new design. By using Liquid Glass materials and the new and updated controls, developers have the opportunity to refresh the design of their apps to make every user interaction even more intuitive and delightful.

The search results include comprehensive information from Apple's official announcements, developer documentation, and design resources for implementing Liquid Glass in applications across Apple platforms.

input.prompt: Extract concrete methodology for SwiftUI design tokens: the token tiers (global/alias/component), how tokens map to SwiftUI code, single source of truth workflow, any specific practices for small teams. Give exact terminology and any code structure claims.

Extract the concrete Figma-to-SwiftUI handoff workflow: what tools/steps, auto layout requirement, CLI workflow, AI-assisted editor integration (Cursor/Windsurf), and any best-practice claims about preparing Figma files for clean SwiftUI output.

content.content:
# SwiftUI Design Tokens: Methodology from the Article

## Token Categories (Not Explicit Tiers)

The article identifies **seven core token categories** rather than traditional global/alias/component tiers:

1. **Spacing**: `xs (4px), sm (8px), md (16px), lg (24px), xl (32px)`
2. **Radius**: `sm (6px), md (12px), lg (20px), card (16px)`
3. **Typography**: Named scales like `title, headline, body, caption`
4. **Colors**: Semantic naming (not visual), e.g., `"background", "surface", "accent"`
5. **Elevation**: Shadow/material values like `card (4px), modal (12px)`
6. **Animation**: Motion durations—`fast (0.15s), standard (0.25s), slow (0.45s)`
7. **Opacity**: Referenced but not detailed

## SwiftUI Code Mapping

The methodology uses **enum-based token definitions**:

> "Never do: `Color.blue`. Instead: `enum AppColor { static let background = Color("Background") }`"

This semantic approach leverages SwiftUI's asset system to handle light/dark modes and accessibility automatically.

## Single Source of Truth Workflow

The article recommends a **theme container injected via environment**:

> ".environment(\\.theme, currentTheme)"

This enables theme switching across system appearance, accessibility settings, and A/B testing without hardcoding values.

## Small Team Practices

The methodology emphasizes **preview-driven validation**: "Design validation becomes instant" through environment-based theme previews, reducing collaboration friction.

**No explicit small-team workflow is detailed.**

# Figma-to-SwiftUI Handoff Workflow

## Tools & Steps

Builder.io's Visual Copilot provides the core workflow:

1. Launch the Visual Copilot Figma plugin
2. Select a layer and preview it
3. Click "Generate code"
4. Copy generated code into Xcode
5. Refine for iOS-specific functionality

The article describes this as enabling developers to "Transform Figma designs into high-quality SwiftUI components with a single click."

## Auto Layout Requirement

Auto layout is mandatory for optimal results. The guide emphasizes: "Implement Figma's auto layout feature to organize elements into responsive hierarchies. This is the single most impactful action for ensuring a smooth import."

## AI Architecture (No CLI/Editor Integration Mentioned)

Visual Copilot uses a multi-stage process:
- Initial model trained on 2+ million data points converts flat designs to code hierarchies
- Open-source compiler (Mitosis) structures the output
- Fine-tuned LLM refines code for specific frameworks

**Note:** The article contains no discussion of CLI workflows, Cursor, Windsurf, or AI-assisted editor integrations.

## Figma Best Practices for Clean Output

Six additional recommendations beyond auto layout:
- Define images explicitly via export
- Group background layers
- Minimize overlaps/intersections
- Avoid translucent effects
- Size text boxes snugly
- Match real-world design dimensions

input.prompt: List the exact 7 steps to build a design system from scratch, with concrete actions in each. Especially UI inventory, foundations (color/type/spacing), patterns, documentation. Any advice on iterative/incremental building.

Extract the design principles Apple communicates for Liquid Glass: hierarchy, harmony, consistency, deference. What concrete design methodology guidance is given for adopting Liquid Glass in an app (macOS especially)? Any advice on not over-using glass, concentric layouts, etc.

content.content:
# 7 Steps to Build a Design System from Scratch

## 1. Evaluate Current UI Inventory
"Review every digital asset and all pre-existing reference materials." Audit color schemes, typography choices, icon libraries, photo libraries, graphics, UI patterns, and page templates. Document inconsistencies across products.

## 2. Get Team Buy-In
Present the efficiency benefits to stakeholders. Highlight how design systems streamline planning, designing, testing, and coding while saving significant time and work throughout the design process.

## 3. Establish Color Palette
Create a master list of all colors used repeatedly. Document primary and secondary colors, text colors, link colors, button colors, and backgrounds with precise HEX, RGBA, or HSL codes and usage guidelines.

## 4. Define Typography Guide
Finalize font choices, text sizes, spacing, and font weights. "Note your preferred text sizes, spaces, fonts, etc., as well as any rules on where and when to use them." Include line heights and custom kerning rules.

## 5. Compile Graphic Assets
Gather standardized icon libraries, photography collections, custom illustrations, and approved branding images. Include design principles and rules for creating new assets (sizes, colors, file formats).

## 6. Build Pattern Library
Document UI patterns with code snippets and development documentation. Organize by function or type, using screenshots and visual cues. Include usage notes explaining implementation specifics.

## 7. House in Central Platform
"Upload the select UI elements in a design system document" using accessible tools like UXPin, enabling drag-and-drop prototyping and developer handoff with complete documentation.

## Incremental Building Note
The article doesn't explicitly address incremental building, though it acknowledges that "most companies compile these only as needed," suggesting phased adoption is feasible.

# Liquid Glass Design Principles & Implementation Guidance

## Core Principles

The article identifies **three** primary principles for Liquid Glass (not four—deference is from older iOS guidelines):

1. **Hierarchy**: "Establish a clear visual hierarchy where controls and interface elements elevate and distinguish the content beneath them."

2. **Harmony**: "Align with the concentric design of the hardware and software to create harmony between interface elements."

3. **Consistency**: "Adopt platform conventions to maintain a consistent design that continuously adapts across window sizes."

## Concrete Implementation Guidance

The article provides four actionable recommendations:

1. **Prioritize clarity through hierarchy** - Use spacing, size, and adaptive component behavior to guide users rather than relying solely on colors or effects.

2. **Think systemically** - Design elements must work consistently across iPhone, iPad, and Mac with adaptive geometry.

3. **Use materials purposefully** - Glass effects and transitions serve functional purposes (signaling depth, feedback, context changes), not decoration.

4. **Rethink native components** - Components are "no longer fixed containers; they are part of the flow" and should adapt based on context and screen size.

## Cautions on Glass Usage

The article emphasizes that designers should choose glass materials that "highlight their product's content rather than obscuring it," implying restraint is essential to avoid visual noise.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about design sprints with focus on compressed, small team, and solo variations:

## Overview of the 5-Day Design Sprint

The design sprint is a five-day process for answering critical business questions through design, rapid design prototyping, and testing ideas. Working together in a sprint, you can shortcut the endless-debate cycle and compress months of time into a single week.

## Solo Design Sprints

If your design team consists of one person, they can still run a Design Sprint. If you are running a solo sprint, some of the stages may take less time because there won't be collective brainstorming and team deliberation, which could take your five-day sprint to four or three days.

## Compressed/Modified Sprints

As the sprint's popularity has increased, some organizations and consultancies are eager to tweak the Design Sprint process, sometimes running abbreviated or compressed sprints — trying to do the same thing in three or four days instead of the classic five.

You don't always need to run a 5-day design sprint. While this is the most common format, you can use this template to split up the sprint into five phases across a longer period, if needed.

## 2024 Modern Modifications

Recent modifications include using AI tools to compress build time, with AI tools like Claude converting concepts into functional prototypes in minutes, using a hybrid approach that maintains the low-tech, focused character of the sprint's early days, then uses AI on Day 4 to convert sketched solutions into interactive experiences faster than traditional development allows.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about prototyping directly in SwiftUI code versus using Figma for designer-developer workflows in 2025:

## Key Differences

**SwiftUI Code Prototyping Advantages:**

SwiftUI puts preview in the Xcode editor with live updates as code is written, with interacting and multi-device previews only a click away. With slightly more work than setting up a prototype of Sketch or Figma images, you can conduct usability tests with full functionality.

SwiftUI custom views are reusable chunks of UI like Figma components, but they don't just stop at buttons or text fields; SwiftUI pushes boundaries with complex elements like tables and lists.

**Figma Advantages:**

While improvements to Figma (developer mode) are helpful, they are no replacement for understanding key concepts of the framework your developers will use. Mastering the interface between design and development is a necessity, and understanding the intricacies of SwiftUI from a Figma perspective is a must for those designing for iOS.

## Current State (2025)

Tools for converting Figma to SwiftUI speed up the layout step which is 10 to 20 percent of app development. State management, navigation, networking, persistence, monetization, accessibility, App Review compliance, and product decisions all stay with developers.

**Alternative Approaches:**

While Figma is a great vector design tool, it is not built for real prototyping on iOS or iPadOS. Unlike other design tools that require converters or plugins to translate design into code, Judo has SwiftUI code creation built in by design.

input.prompt: Extract the argument and concrete methodology for prototyping directly in SwiftUI instead of/alongside Figma. What are the practical steps, benefits for a solo dev/designer, and any caveats? Any specific workflow tips (Xcode Previews, reusable views mirroring components).

Extract concrete guidance on 1-day, 2-day, 3-day compressed design sprints: when each works, what gets cut, tradeoffs, and recommendations for small teams. Give specifics on what activities remain in shortened sprints.

content.content:
# SwiftUI Prototyping: Direct Alternative to Figma

## Core Argument

Barker advocates SwiftUI prototyping because it bridges the gap between design mockups and testable reality. His key position: "With slightly more work than setting up a prototype of Sketch or Figma images, you can be conducting usability tests with full functionality." This approach validates design decisions before expensive development.

## Practical Methodology

**Direct implementation steps:**
- Layout UI using SwiftUI containers (Lists, Sheets, etc.)
- Integrate sample/fake data into working prototypes
- Deploy to physical devices or TestFlight for real user testing
- Iterate based on actual user behavior rather than theoretical feedback

**Concrete benefit cited:** A horizontally scrolling list interaction that failed in Invision prototyping (users couldn't interact properly) would require "only a few lines of code" in SwiftUI—making complex interactions testable without full app development.

## Solo Designer/Developer Advantages

- Eliminates complexity barriers; "the amount of complexity is night and day" versus Storyboards
- Enables designers to contribute front-end layout without production-ready code
- Reduces wasted development time by validating interactions preemptively
- Real device testing validates design quality before handoff

## Key Caveats

- SwiftUI still has feature gaps (though improving annually)
- Contrived testing scenarios emerge with limited static product data
- Prototypes become "quite large and complex" scaling to full shopping flows
- Requires learning curve beyond typical design tool expertise

## Workflow Tips

Barker references Xcode's preview functionality implicitly but emphasizes building "a working prototype that can at least be installed on an iPhone" for authentic user feedback—the genuine validation metric.

# Compressed Design Sprint Guidance

## Key Finding
The article strongly discourages shortened sprints for most organizations. The author states: **"If you aren't prototyping and testing, it's NOT a Design Sprint."**

## What Gets Cut in 3-4 Day Sprints
When compressed, teams typically eliminate:
- Full prototyping day
- User testing phase
- Adequate time for problem exploration and team alignment

## When Shorter Sprints *Might* Work

**3-Day Sprints:**
- Only for highly mature organizations with extensive design experience
- Jake Knapp warns: **"Three days is super intense, and I wouldn't sign up for it myself"**
- Requires significant pre-existing resources (designers, researchers, design systems)
- Google has "earned the right" through years of specialization

**4-Day Sprints:**
- Feasible only with experienced facilitators or teams with sprint background
- Requires substantial pre-work before the sprint begins
- Still considered challenging by creator Jake Knapp

## Critical Tradeoffs
- **Loss of learning moments** - Skipping testing removes "authentic direction" from actual users
- **Reduced innovation** - Teams uncover less bold ideas with compressed timelines
- **Hidden costs** - What appears as 3-4 days often expands to 8+ days when pre-work and behind-scenes prototyping are included
- **Weakened ownership** - Distributed work (design team prototypes separately) reduces team buy-in

## Recommendation for Small Teams
**Start with the full 5-day format.** The author emphasizes: **"If you're new to Design Sprints, I strongly recommend starting with a five-day sprint."** Only customize after mastering the original process.

input.prompt: List Nielsen's 10 usability heuristics by name, and note the publication/update date. These are classic long-standing UX evaluation principles.
Extract what Figma Code Connect for SwiftUI does: how it links SwiftUI components to Figma components, prop mapping, variant mapping, and how it appears in Dev Mode. Concrete workflow steps.

content.content:
# Nielsen's 10 Usability Heuristics

**Publication Date:** April 24, 1994  
**Last Updated:** January 30, 2024

## The 10 Heuristics:

1. **Visibility of System Status** — "The design should always keep users informed about what is going on, through appropriate feedback within a reasonable amount of time."

2. **Match Between the System and the Real World** — Systems should employ familiar terminology and follow real-world conventions rather than internal jargon.

3. **User Control and Freedom** — Designs must provide clear emergency exits so users can undo unwanted actions without extended processes.

4. **Consistency and Standards** — Systems should follow platform and industry conventions to prevent confusion across different contexts.

5. **Error Prevention** — The best designs prevent problems proactively rather than relying solely on error recovery mechanisms.

6. **Recognition Rather than Recall** — Interfaces should minimize memory demands by making options and information visible throughout.

7. **Flexibility and Efficiency of Use** — Systems should accommodate both novice and expert users through customizable shortcuts and personalization.

8. **Aesthetic and Minimalist Design** — Interfaces should focus on essential content and features without irrelevant or rarely-needed information.

9. **Help Users Recognize, Diagnose, and Recover from Errors** — Error messages should use plain language, identify problems clearly, and suggest constructive solutions.

10. **Help and Documentation** — Systems should provide searchable, task-focused documentation presented in context when needed.

# Figma Code Connect for SwiftUI

## Core Functionality

Figma Code Connect for SwiftUI bridges design and development by linking SwiftUI components to their Figma counterparts. It displays accurate code snippets in Dev Mode that dynamically reflect design properties.

## Key Workflow Steps

**1. Setup Requirements**
Add Code Connect as a dependency in your `Package.swift` file, specifying the Figma package for relevant targets.

**2. Property Mapping**
Use property wrappers to connect design properties to code:
- `@FigmaString` for text values
- `@FigmaBoolean` for toggles
- `@FigmaEnum` for variant selections
- `@FigmaInstance` for nested instances
- `@FigmaChildren` for child layers

Example: *"@FigmaString("Text Content") var label: String"* maps a Figma text property to a Swift variable.

**3. Variant Mapping**
When a single Figma component represents multiple code components, create separate connection structs with variant specifications: `let variant = ["Type": "Primary"]`

**4. Conditional Modifiers**
Use `figmaApply` to apply modifiers based on design states, with optional `elseApply` fallback.

**5. Dev Mode Display**
Connected code appears automatically when inspecting component instances, updating based on property changes.

**6. Xcode Integration**
Use connection structs directly as preview definitions without duplicating examples.

input.angle: 设计流程方法论——design system 从零构建、Figma→SwiftUI handoff、低保真→高保真迭代、双钻/design sprint 在桌面端与 solo 小团队的裁剪应用
input.summary: 对于 MAformac 这种 solo/小团队、纯端侧 macOS SwiftUI 演示助手,主流方法论可以大幅裁剪但有几条不能省。第一,design system 从零建的公认序是"先盘存 UI 现状 → 定基础层(色/字/间距 tokens) → 再搭 pattern 库 → 最后集中沉淀文档",且必须增量式一个 pattern 一个 pattern 建,没有"完成态"只有迭代。第二,token 化是 SSOT 的关键:业界普遍用 global/alias/component 三层语义命名;落到 SwiftUI 的成熟做法是用 enum + Asset Catalog 承载颜色(自动接管明暗/无障碍)、把主题经 environment 注入,配合 Xcode Preview 做即时验证——这对 solo 尤其高效。第三,Figma→SwiftUI handoff 有两条路:AI 生成(Visily/Builder Visual Copilot/Trace/Codia)只解决约 10–20% 的布局搭建,状态/导航/无障碍/产品决策仍归开发者;Auto Layout 是决定导入质量的"单一最关键动作";Figma Code Connect 用 @FigmaString 等属性包装器把真实 SwiftUI 组件挂到 Figma 组件,在 Dev Mode 显示活代码。第四,越来越多人主张 solo 直接在 SwiftUI 里做可交互原型(接假数据、上真机/TestFlight 测),跳过 Figma 中间层,因为复杂交互(横向滚动列表等)在代码里几行就能真实验证。第五,过程框架都要裁:低保真→高保真的转场信号是"你能明确说出正在测的问题、且该问题需要更高保真才能答";双钻对 solo 可压到几天但 Discover/Define 不能整段砍;design sprint 单人可压到 3–4 天,但"不做原型+测试就不叫 sprint"。最后,Liquid Glass(macOS 26 Tahoe)时代的设计准则强调 hierarchy/harmony/consistency 三原则、材质要"服务内容而非遮蔽内容",叠加 Nielsen 十大启发式这类长期原则,构成演示 App 视觉与可用性的底盘。

findings.claim: Design system 从零构建的标准 7 步序:①盘存现有 UI(审计所有颜色/字体/图标/模式并记录不一致)②争取团队认同 ③定色板(主/次/文本/链接/按钮/背景,附精确 HEX/RGBA/HSL 与用法)④定排版(字号/字重/行高/间距及使用规则)⑤汇总图形资产 ⑥建 pattern 库(带代码片段与文档)⑦集中沉淀到一个平台便于原型与 handoff。基础层(色/字/间距)先于 pattern 库。
findings.source_title: Build A Design System from Scratch in 7 Steps | UXPin
findings.notes: 已 WebFetch 逐条核验。适配 solo:第②步团队认同可省,其余序保留。

findings.claim: design system 是迭代物不是一次性交付——'没有完成的设计系统,只有不断迭代';pattern 库应先定最佳架构再一个一个建,不要一次搭全部。业界普遍报告用 design system 后交付更快、设计类 bug 显著减少(约快 34%、bug 减约 47%),某项目文档化耗时约 3.5 个月(1.5 月全职+2 月半投入)。
findings.source_title: Design Systems: Step-by-Step Guide to Creating Your Own | UXPin
findings.notes: 34%/47%/3.5月为搜索摘要聚合数字,未逐页核验,决策慎用;'增量一个一个建、无完成态'为多源一致的定性结论,可信度高。

findings.claim: Design token 业界三层结构:①Global tokens=原始值(hex 色值、像素尺寸)②Alias/semantic tokens=语义引用(如 color-primary 指向 global)③Component tokens=组件作用域(如 button-background-color 指向 alias)。token 是'命名的语义值,表达意图而非实现',是设计系统的单一事实源。
findings.source_title: What Are Design Tokens? A Complete Guide (2026) | UXPin
findings.notes: 搜索摘要确认三层命名(global/alias/component);MAformac 若做 tokens 建议至少 global+semantic 两层即可,component 层 solo 可缓。

findings.claim: SwiftUI 落地 design token 的生产级做法:分 7 类(spacing/radius/typography/colors/elevation/animation/opacity),用 enum 承载(禁止直接写 Color.blue,改 enum AppColor { static let background = Color("Background") } 交给 Asset Catalog 自动处理明暗与无障碍),主题用 .environment(\.theme, currentTheme) 注入,配合 Xcode Preview 做即时设计验证。spacing 示例 xs4/sm8/md16/lg24/xl32,radius sm6/md12/lg20/card16,animation fast0.15/standard0.25/slow0.45s。
findings.source_title: SwiftUI Design Tokens & Theming System (Production-Scale) - DEV Community
findings.notes: 已 WebFetch;enum+Asset Catalog+environment 注入+Preview 验证这套对 solo 极高效,直接可套用到 MAformac 的卡片亮暗态。

findings.claim: Figma Code Connect for SwiftUI 把真实 SwiftUI 组件与 Figma 组件双向绑定:在 Package.swift 加依赖,用属性包装器映射设计属性(@FigmaString 文本、@FigmaBoolean 开关、@FigmaEnum 变体、@FigmaInstance 嵌套、@FigmaChildren 子层),变体用 let variant=["Type":"Primary"] 分派,figmaApply/elseApply 按状态加修饰符;连好后 Dev Mode 自动显示随属性变化的活代码,连接 struct 可直接当 Preview 用。
findings.source_title: Connecting SwiftUI components | Figma Developer Docs
findings.notes: 已 WebFetch;这是'代码是真实源、Figma 显示真实代码'的反向 handoff,适合已有 SwiftUI 组件库时保持设计-代码一致。

findings.claim: Figma→SwiftUI 的 AI 生成 handoff(Builder Visual Copilot):流程=开插件→选层预览→Generate code→拷进 Xcode→按 iOS 细化;'启用 Figma Auto Layout 把元素组织成响应式层级是保证顺畅导入的单一最关键动作',另有 6 条:显式导出图片、合并背景层、减少重叠、避免半透明效果、文本框贴合、用真实设计尺寸。AI 用多阶段(200 万+数据点模型转层级→Mitosis 编译器→微调 LLM 精炼框架代码)。
findings.notes: 已 WebFetch;'Auto Layout 是导入质量单一最关键动作'是可执行的强约束。同类工具还有 Trace、Codia AI(搜索见到)。

findings.claim: Figma→SwiftUI 转换工具只加速了'布局搭建'这一步(约占 App 开发 10–20%);状态管理、导航、网络、持久化、变现、无障碍、App Review 合规、产品决策仍全部留在开发者手里。因此 handoff 工具是脚手架不是省略开发。
findings.notes: 搜索摘要确认;对 MAformac 的意义:别指望 Figma 生成可省掉 SwiftUI 逻辑,原型价值在交互与状态而非静态布局。

findings.claim: solo 开发者可跳过 Figma 中间层、直接用 SwiftUI 做可交互原型:用 List/Sheet 等容器搭 UI、灌假数据、部署真机/TestFlight 做真实可用性测试。理由是复杂交互(如横向滚动列表在 InVision 里测不出来)在 SwiftUI 里'几行代码'就能真实验证;比 Storyboard 复杂度低得多,设计者也能贡献前端布局。代价:SwiftUI 仍有能力空缺、原型规模化后会变大变复杂、有学习曲线。
findings.notes: 已 WebFetch;高度契合 MAformac(solo+SwiftUI 一套+要现场演真交互)——建议高保真阶段直接在代码里迭代而非 Figma 走查。Design+Code、Judo 亦持类似'代码即原型'立场。

findings.claim: 低保真→高保真的转场判据:'当你能清晰说出正在测什么问题、且该问题需要更高保真才能回答时,才升保真;在此之前保持低保真快速迭代'。策略序=低保真验概念→中保真解结构问题→高保真定稿并备 handoff;可在流程中途改某个 artifact 只重跑下游步骤而不推倒重来,以此快迭代不丢上下文。
findings.source_title: Low Fidelity vs. High Fidelity Prototypes | Miro
findings.notes: 搜索摘要确认;可作 MAformac 卡片/演示界面的保真升级门槛,避免过早打磨视觉。

findings.claim: Double Diamond 可裁给 solo/小团队:框架是参考不是死板照搬,已被用于几天的单人项目到 3–6 个月的小团队项目;资源受限时可做'轻量 Discover/Define'——限定而非整段跳过发现期,几天有针对性的证据能避免几个月做错东西;定明确的决策日期和可测指标。
findings.source_title: Understanding the Double Diamond design process | Dovetail
findings.notes: 搜索摘要确认;对 MAformac 的裁剪启示:发现期不砍到 0,但可压到几天并锁死决策日。

findings.claim: design sprint 的压缩规则:单人 sprint 可行,因缺少集体脑暴/团队讨论,5 天常可压到 3–4 天;但更短 sprint 有风险——'不做原型+测试就不叫 Design Sprint',3 天版仅适合高成熟度团队(Jake Knapp 本人也说'三天极其硬核,我自己不会报名'),压缩常把砍掉的原型/测试挪到会前而实际膨胀到 8+ 天。新手建议先跑完整 5 天再裁。2024 新变体:用 AI(如 Claude)在 Day 4 把手绘方案快速转成可交互原型。
findings.source_title: 1-Day, 2-Day, and 3-Day Design Sprints. Do they work? | Voltage Control
findings.notes: 已 WebFetch;核心可迁移原则=可压缩但'原型+测试'环节不能砍。AI 转原型这条直接对应 MAformac 用 Claude/SwiftUI 快速出可演版本。

findings.claim: Liquid Glass(macOS 26 Tahoe / WWDC25 起)采用设计三原则:Hierarchy(控件与界面元素抬升并区分其下内容,靠间距/尺寸/自适应行为引导而非只靠颜色特效)、Harmony(对齐硬件与软件的同心/concentric 几何)、Consistency(遵循平台约定并随窗口尺寸持续自适应);材质要'服务功能'(示深度/反馈/上下文变化)而非装饰,应选'凸显内容而非遮蔽内容'的玻璃材质;组件不再是固定容器而是流的一部分。
findings.source_title: Liquid Glass: Redefining design through Hierarchy, Harmony and Consistency | Create with Swift
findings.notes: 已 WebFetch;直接约束 MAformac macOS 演示界面的视觉语言——克制用玻璃、内容优先。Apple 已随 WWDC25 更新 SwiftUI/UIKit/AppKit 的 Liquid Glass API 与设计资源。

findings.claim: Nielsen 十大可用性启发式(1994 发布,2024-01-30 最近更新)仍是通用评估底盘:①系统状态可见 ②系统贴合现实 ③用户控制与自由(明确的撤销/退出)④一致性与标准 ⑤错误预防 ⑥识别优于回忆 ⑦灵活与高效(快捷方式)⑧美学与极简 ⑨帮助用户识别/诊断/恢复错误 ⑩帮助与文档。
findings.source_title: 10 Usability Heuristics for User Interface Design | Nielsen Norman Group
findings.notes: 已 WebFetch;经典长期原则(非 2024-26 新料但 2024 已更新)。对 5 分钟炸场演示尤其相关的是①状态可见(反应快看得见)与⑧极简、⑨错误恢复(不崩/优雅兜底)。
